understanding.function: Nuclear fuel reprocessing system for extracting fissile material (uranium and plutonium) from spent nuclear fuel waste, capable of processing multiple fuel types (UOX, MOX, ERU/REU) with 1800 tHM/year capacity
understanding.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing and fissile material recovery
constraints: Product purity standards for downstream fuel fabrication
uncertainties: Specific technical processes and equipment details not defined
Exact safety and regulatory requirements beyond ALARA principle unclear
Interface specifications with upstream waste reception and downstream fuel fabrication systems undefined
Specific product purity thresholds and quality metrics not quantified
Emergency response and accident scenarios not detailed
Maintenance and decommissioning procedures not specified

candidates.name: PUREX Process
candidates.description: Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction process using solvent extraction with tributyl phosphate (TBP) for separating uranium and plutonium from spent nuclear fuel
candidates.function_alignment: Direct match - established industrial process for uranium/plutonium extraction from spent fuel
candidates.notes: Most widely used commercial reprocessing technology, proven at industrial scale

candidates.name: PUREX (Plutonium Uranium Redox Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Industry-standard aqueous reprocessing method using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene to separate uranium and plutonium from fission products through solvent extraction. Widely implemented at facilities like La Hague and Sellafield with proven multi-fuel type capability.
candidates.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing
candidates.function_alignment: Direct match - established technology for uranium/plutonium extraction from spent fuel with demonstrated high-capacity processing
candidates.notes: Most mature and widely deployed reprocessing technology, suitable for UOX, MOX, and enriched uranium fuels

candidates.name: COEX (Co-Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Advanced reprocessing technology that co-extracts uranium and plutonium together rather than separately, designed to enhance proliferation resistance while maintaining separation efficiency. Developed by AREVA/Orano for next-generation facilities.
candidates.function_alignment: High alignment - specifically designed for proliferation-resistant fissile material recovery with multi-fuel capability
candidates.notes: Enhanced security features while maintaining processing efficiency, suitable for modern regulatory frameworks

candidates.name: GANEX (Group Actinide Extraction) Process
candidates.description: Advanced partitioning process for simultaneous extraction of uranium, plutonium, and minor actinides using selective extractants. Enables comprehensive actinide recovery for advanced fuel cycles and waste minimization.
candidates.function_alignment: Moderate alignment - broader scope than required but includes target fissile material extraction with advanced separation capabilities
candidates.notes: More comprehensive than basic U/Pu extraction, includes minor actinides for advanced fuel cycle applications

candidates.name: Modular Reprocessing Plant Design
candidates.description: Standardized modular approach to reprocessing facility design enabling scalable capacity, enhanced safety through compartmentalization, and simplified maintenance. Incorporates remote handling and automated process control systems.
candidates.function_alignment: High alignment - addresses capacity requirements, multi-fuel processing, and operational availability through modular design principles
candidates.notes: Enables meeting 1800 tHM/year capacity through scalable modules with enhanced safety and maintenance features

candidates.name: Advanced Centrifugal Contactors
candidates.description: High-efficiency solvent extraction equipment using centrifugal force to achieve rapid phase separation and mixing. Provides compact footprint, reduced solvent inventory, and improved process control for reprocessing applications.
candidates.function_alignment: High alignment - critical equipment for efficient uranium/plutonium extraction with reduced facility footprint and enhanced safety
candidates.notes: Key enabling technology for modern reprocessing facilities, reduces solvent holdup and improves process efficiency

candidates.name: Integrated Safeguards and Security System
candidates.description: Comprehensive monitoring and control system combining IAEA safeguards requirements with physical protection measures. Includes real-time nuclear material accountancy, process monitoring, and automated reporting capabilities.
candidates.function_alignment: Moderate alignment - essential supporting system for fissile material handling but not directly involved in extraction process
candidates.notes: Critical for regulatory compliance and material accountability in fissile material processing operations

candidates.name: Hot Cell Remote Handling Systems
candidates.description: Automated and semi-automated remote manipulation systems for handling highly radioactive materials in shielded environments. Includes master-slave manipulators, robotic systems, and specialized tooling for fuel disassembly and processing operations.
candidates.function_alignment: High alignment - essential infrastructure for safe handling of spent fuel and maintaining ALARA principles during processing
candidates.notes: Critical enabling technology for safe spent fuel processing operations with high radiation fields

candidates.name: Vitrification and Waste Form Technology
candidates.description: Process for immobilizing high-level radioactive waste (fission products and minor actinides) in borosilicate glass matrix for long-term storage and disposal. Integrated with reprocessing operations for comprehensive waste management.
candidates.function_alignment: Moderate alignment - complementary technology for managing waste streams from fissile material extraction processes
candidates.notes: Essential downstream process for managing high-level waste generated during reprocessing operations
